title: DOCKER Service Environment Variable Validation with Complex Patterns

statement.markdown: Write a function that takes a Docker Compose environment variables object as input and returns an array of all environment variables that do not match the following pattern: `KEY=VALUE`. The pattern allows for the presence of whitespace before or after the equals sign but requires that the key starts with a letter and only contains letters, digits, or underscores. The values can contain any characters. You may assume that the input object will only contain environment variables in the correct format.
statement.inputFormat: Object where keys are environment variable names and values are their corresponding values
statement.outputFormat: Array of environment variable names that do not match the specified pattern
statement.notes: You may use regular expressions to validate the pattern

constraints.description: Number of environment variables in the input object

examples.explanation: The environment variable names 'KEY=  VALUE' and 'KEY=VALUE ' do not match the pattern because they contain whitespace before or after the equals sign respectively.

hints.text: Use a regular expression to validate the pattern
Make sure to handle cases where the key starts with a letter and contains only letters, digits, or underscores

editorial.title: DOCKER Service Environment Variable Validation with Complex Patterns
editorial.intuition: You can use a regular expression to validate the pattern and then filter out the environment variables that do not match it.
editorial.bruteForce: You can manually write a function that checks each environment variable one by one to see if it matches the pattern.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to validate the pattern and then filter out the environment variables that do not match it.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to validate the pattern and then filter out the environment variables that do not match it. You can use the `match` method of the string to check if the pattern matches.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: You can use a unit test to prove that the function works correctly.
editorial.dryRun: You can use a sample input to test the function and see if it returns the correct output.
commonMistakes: Not handling cases where the key starts with a letter and contains only letters, digits, or underscores
Not using a regular expression to validate the pattern

starterCode.javascript: // Write a function that takes a Docker Compose environment variables object as input and returns an array of all environment variables that do not match the pattern: KEY=VALUE.
